text: There is a peculiar sense of observation, of being both present and set apart. Through the glass, I see others gathered, learning or sharing quietly, as I stand on the threshold, separated by architecture and silence. The corridors echo with institutional order, but against this backdrop, moments of connection and quiet curiosity emerge. I feel gently estranged—invited to watch, but not to belong, which tugs at the corners of my mind with questions of inclusion and solitude.
